title: Speed Run: Data Frames and Factors

questions.question: Factors can store both numeric and character data.
questions.explanation: Factors can have numeric levels (e.g., factor(c(1,2,1))) but they are stored as integers with labels.

questions.question: The `ifelse()` function works element-wise on vectors.
questions.explanation: Yes, `ifelse(test, yes, no)` returns a vector of the same length as test, with elements chosen from yes/no.

questions.question: A for loop in R always executes at least once.
questions.explanation: If the vector being iterated over is empty, the loop body never executes.

questions.question: Functions in R can return only one object.
questions.explanation: A function can return a list containing multiple objects, effectively returning multiple values.

questions.question: `break` is used to skip the current iteration of a loop.
questions.explanation: `break` exits the loop entirely. To skip to the next iteration, use `next`.

questions.question: In R, you can define a function without any arguments.
